differences.title: 1. CORE LANGUAGE
differences.leftDesc: Relies on JavaScript and Vue.js ecosystem for its core functionality and reactivity.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles Svelte code into efficient imperative JavaScript at build time.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY
differences.leftDesc: Uses Vue's built-in reactivity system based on Proxies for state management.
differences.rightDesc: Reactivity is handled by the compiler, no virtual DOM at runtime.

differences.title: 3. SERVER-SIDE
differences.leftDesc: Built with Node.js, offering flexibility and a large module ecosystem.
differences.rightDesc: Uses adapters for various deployment targets (Node, Vercel, Cloudflare).

differences.title: 4. ROUTING
differences.leftDesc: Convention-driven file-based routing with dynamic routing capabilities.
differences.rightDesc: Convention-driven file-based routing, similar to Nuxt.js.

differences.title: 5. DATA FETCHING
differences.leftDesc: Provides methods like `asyncData` and `fetch` for fetching data.

differences.title: 6. PERFORMANCE
differences.leftDesc: Leverages Vue's runtime performance and ecosystem.
differences.rightDesc: Compiles to minimal JavaScript, leading to smaller bundle sizes.

differences.title: 7. ECOSYSTEM
differences.leftDesc: Benefits from the extensive and mature Vue.js ecosystem.
differences.rightDesc: A rapidly growing ecosystem centered around Svelte's unique approach.
